metadata.name: Dockhand
metadata.description: Modern Docker management UI with Compose stack support, Git-based stack deployments, and optional OIDC authentication.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Dockhand is a modern Docker management UI with Compose stack support, Git-based stack deployments, and optional OIDC authentication.

## Prerequisites
- This container needs access to the Docker socket, so deploy it only on a host you trust.
- This template uses a hardcoded named volume `dockhand_data:/app/data` for the simplest setup.
- If you switch to PostgreSQL, provide a reachable external database before first start.
- If you enable Traefik, make sure the external network already exists.

## Deploy
- Render `compose.yaml`, review the database mode and access method, then start it with `docker compose up -d`.
- Open Dockhand and finish the initial configuration. Authentication is disabled on first launch until you enable it in the UI.

## Post-Install
- Add the local Docker environment and verify Dockhand can reach the socket.
- If you see Docker socket permission errors, adjust the container user or group mapping to match your host.
- This simple named-volume setup is fine for basic use, but if your Dockhand-managed stacks rely on relative bind mounts you should move to a matching host-path `DATA_DIR` layout later.
<%- if encryption_key %>
- `ENCRYPTION_KEY` is set from a secret. Dockhand expects a base64-encoded 32-byte AES-256 key; keep this value stable or encrypted credentials cannot be recovered.
<%- endif %>
<%- if disable_local_login %>
- `DISABLE_LOCAL_LOGIN` is enabled in this compose file. Only use this after SSO is fully working so you do not lock yourself out.
<%- endif %>
<%- if puid or pgid %>
- `PUID` and `PGID` are optional runtime overrides for Docker socket and volume permission alignment. On Linux, check them with `id -u` and `id -g`.
<%- endif %>

## References
- [Dockhand](https://dockhand.pro/)
- [Manual](https://dockhand.pro/manual/)

items.name: encryption_key
items.title: Encryption Key
items.description: Base64-encoded 32-byte AES-256 key used for credential encryption.

items.name: puid
items.title: PUID
items.description: Optional user ID for Dockhand. On Linux check with `id -u`.

items.name: pgid
items.title: PGID
items.description: Optional group ID for Dockhand. On Linux check with `id -g`.

items.name: database_host
items.title: Database Host
items.description: Hostname used for the external PostgreSQL database.

items.name: database_port
items.title: Database Port
items.description: Port used for the external PostgreSQL database.

items.name: disable_local_login
items.title: Disable Local Login
items.description: when SSO correctly setup, not recommended as first deployment
